TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AS EF-1 NEAR U.S. 1 AND MOVED RAPIDLY NORTHEAST, WEAKENING TO AN EF-0 BEFORE LIFTING OVER INTRACOASTAL WATERWAY NEAR SE PALM STREET AT 715 AM EST. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WITH TRUNKS SNAPPED AND/OR UPROOTED. DAMAGE TO ROOFS AND CAR FROM TREES.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS NUMEROUS PINE TREES FALLEN, DOWNED PORTIONS OF FENCES, AND A FEW STREET SIGNS BENT IN THE VICINITY OF POINCIANA BOULEVARD AND CYPRESS PARKWAY WHERE HAIL WAS PILING UP IN DRIFTS UP TO 1 FOOT DEEP AT THE PEAK OF THE SEVERE THUNDERSTORM.
HIGH WINDS PEELED OFF WHAT APPEARS TO BE PART OF A CORRUGATED METAL ROOF AT A CAR WASH AND DROPPED IT ONTO A PARKED VEHICLE, DAMAGING IT. PUBLIC REPORT RELAYED VIA WESH-TV METEOROLOGIST.
HIGH WINDS PEELED OFF WHAT APPEARS TO BE PART OF A CORRUGATED METAL ROOF AT A CAR WASH AND DROPPED IT ONTO A PARKED VEHICLE, DAMAGING IT. PUBLIC REPORT RELAYED VIA WESH-TV METEOROLOGIST.

QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN FELLSMERE. REPORT RELAYED BY EMERGENCY MANAGER.
1800 BLOCK/14 AVE SW, DAMAGE TO TREES, 1 HOME, POWER LINES DOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH 0.25 MILE LENGTH, 100 YARD WIDTH, WINDS 80-90 MPH. TWO HOMES WITH MAJOR DAMAGE, 16 HOMES WITH MINOR DAMAGE MAINLY TO ROOFS. SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED, 2 TREES UPROOTED. ESTIMATED LOSS 395,000 DOLLARS.

NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED EF-1 TORNADO WITH PATH LENGTH 0.5 MILE LONG, 75 YARDS WIDE, WINDS 80-90 MPH. APPROXIMATELY 15 HOMES DAMAGED, TREES SNAPPED, FENCES AND POOL ENCLOSURES DESTROYED, PORTIONS OF SEVERAL ROOFS PEELED BACK.
DAMAGE TO WAREHOUSE ROOF NEAR INTERSECTION OF KINGS HWY AND ANGLE RD. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. REPORT RELAYED FROM WPTV.
DAMAGE TO AROUND 20 HOMES, 2 SEVERELY DAMAGE IN 1500 BLOCK OF 19TH PLACE SW, IN VERO HIGHLANDS. ALSO DAMAGE TO CARS, BOATS, FENCES, POOL ENCLOSURES, CARPORTS AND LARGE TREES DOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

NWS SURVEY TEAM HAS CONFIRMED A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN LAKEWOOD PARK WITH A WELL-DEFINED DAMAGE PATH. INFORMATION ABOUT WIND SPEEDS, PATH LENGTH AND WIDTH, INCLUDING MORE SPECIFIC INFORMATION ON DAMAGE WILL FOLLOW WHEN THE SURVEY HAS BEEN COMPLETED.

*** 1 INJ *** DAMAGE TO HOMES IN 6500 BLOCK OF LEE BLVD, 8100-8300 BLOCKS OF FORT PIERCE BLVD, 4-5 TRAILERS DAMAGED IN ROAD RUNNER TRAILER PARK WITH ONE INJURY, POWER LINES ON SEMI IN FRONT OF THE ROAD RUNNER TRAILER PARK. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH APPROXIMATE WIND SPEEDS OF 65 TO 75 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN A TRAILER PARK JUST WEST OF THE SAINT LUCIE COUNTY AIRPORT. THE TORNADO PRODUCED MOSTLY SUPERFICIAL WIND DAMAGE TO SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES, BLEW OVER 3-4 RVS, AND SNAPPED ONE LARGE TREE. FROM THERE, THE TORNADO TRAVELED APPROXIMATELY 0.75 MILES SE TO AN OPEN WAREHOUSE AT A CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, WHERE IT THEN LIFTED. SEVERAL NEARBY LOCATIONS REPORTED PERIPHERAL WIND DAMAGE.
